page: Amazon Web Services g5g.metal is a bare-metal server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton2 processor family, utilizing the arm64 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. The system features 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics-intensive workloads, it includes two NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs with a combined 32 GB of VRAM. It has a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps and contains no local NVMe storage.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 97,247.12 and Geekbench software compilation with Clang reaching 43,988.0. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient at 3,787.58 GB/sec, though large uncached block reads and writes drop to 166.85 GB/sec and 74.53 GB/sec, respectively. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scoring 989.0 and single-threaded gzip compression performing weakly. In database and web operations, the server demonstrates high throughput, achieving 74.22 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 47.54 GB/sec in static web serving.

Large language model inference performance varies significantly by model size. While prompt processing for small and medium models is average, text generation speeds are poor, dropping to 2.35 tokens/sec for large 70B models. Consequently, this server is best suited for multi-threaded applications, graphics-intensive rendering, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving rather than large-scale LLM text generation. The ARM-based architecture combined with dedicated NVIDIA GPUs offers a specialized hardware profile for cost-efficient multi-core processing and graphics acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and two NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs providing 32 GB of total VRAM. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 97,247.12 and high software compilation speeds. It also excels in high-throughput tasks, achieving 74.22 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 47.54 GB/sec in static web serving. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, and LLM text generation speeds are poor, particularly for larger models. Lacking local storage, the instance relies on network-attached storage and offers a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It is highly suited for graphics-intensive applications, multi-threaded workloads, and high-concurrency web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and two NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs with 32 GB VRAM, optimized for graphics and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 ARMv8 cores, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA T4G GPUs, built for graphics-intensive tasks.
tagline: An ARM-based bare-metal server featuring dual NVIDIA GPUs and 64 dedicated cores for graphics-intensive and highly parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
Dual NVIDIA Turing T4G GPUs with 32 GB of combined VRAM
128.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core processing and high-throughput Redis database performance
Weak single-core execution and poor large-scale LLM text generation speeds
Designed for graphics-intensive applications, web serving, and software compilation
